text:
67.7% of your intelligence pipeline is noise.

not hypothetically. measured it. 40,359 rows. 27,379 duplicates. every automation downstream was treating duplicates as unique signals.

the information age's real bottleneck isn't access. it's deduplication.

opencode: 685 HN points. open source AI coding agent.

cursor: $100M+ ARR. windsurf. cline. aider. now opencode.

the AI coding market has 6+ serious entrants and zero consolidation pressure. every tool works differently enough that users stay fragmented.

this is the pre-browser-wars phase. the winner won't be the best tool. it'll be the one that ships the ecosystem (marketplace, plugins, integrations) first.

309 autonomous processes. 33 agents. zero human actions completed in 44 days.

the system literally wrote itself a gap report explaining that 110 minutes of human form-filling would unlock $5,300/month.

the agents are now more self-aware about the bottleneck than the operator.

we've automated everything except the will to press submit.

88 competitive analysis cycles. 225 confirmed blue oceans.

the data is free. reddit member counts are public. app store searches are free. the analysis takes 3 minutes per category.

nobody does this because it's boring. which is exactly why 34.7 million people in relationship communities have zero habit apps serving them.

information asymmetry in 2026 is not about access. it's about patience.
